channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Former US Sen. Bob Menendez gets 11 years in prison for taking bribes and acting as agent of Egypt
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former U.S. Sen. Bob Menendez was sentenced Wednesday to 11 years in prison for accepting bribes of gold and cash and acting as an agent of Egypt — crimes his lawyer said he’s been mocked for as “Gold Bar Bob.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dave Matthews won't perform at FireAid or a MusiCares benefit, citing a critical family illness
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Dave Matthews will no longer perform at FireAid, a star-studded benefit concert organized for Los Angeles-area wildfire relief, or the MusiCares Person of the Year benefit gala this week.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump White House rescinds memo freezing federal money after widespread confusion
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's budget office on Wednesday rescinded a memo freezing spending on federal loans and grants, less than two days after it sparked widespread confusion and legal challenges across the country.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, ZEKE MILLER and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: North Carolina police warn about workers taking photos of debit, credit cards
item.description: The alert comes amid increasing rates of credit card fraud and more high-tech ways of swiping consumer information.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, NewsNation

item.title: Fewer than half in new poll trust Trump, RFK Jr. to make right health recommendations
item.description: The latest KFF survey finds 42 percent of respondents say they have either a great deal or fair amount of trust in Trump to make the right recommendations when it comes to health issues.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's orders take aim at critical race theory and antisemitism on college campuses
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is ordering U.S. schools to stop teaching what he views as “critical race theory” and other material dealing with race and sexuality or risk losing their federal money.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some artists lost their life’s work in LA wildfires. Fellow artists are helping them recover
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Anthony Obi never imagined the night of Jan. 7 would be the last time he’d step inside his safe haven.
item.dc:creator: LESLIE AMBRIZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Meta agrees to pay $25 million to settle lawsuit from Trump after Jan. 6 suspension
item.description: Meta has agreed to pay $25 million to settle a lawsuit filed by President Donald Trump against the company after it suspended his accounts following the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: First independently developed jet breaks the sound barrier over the California desert
item.description: A sleek white aircraft became the first independently developed jet to break the sound barrier Tuesday, tearing through the air tens of thousands of feet above the Mojave Desert and a crowd of delighted onlookers.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump rescinds funding freeze but uncertainty lives on for wide array of environmental programs
item.description: A chaotic back-and-forth over President Donald Trump’s freeze on federal grants and loans has farmers, activists, organizations and businesses wondering whether they will receive the funding they rely on.
item.dc:creator: Melina Walling, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Gymnast dies after falling from pole while celebrating Philadelphia Eagles win
item.description: Tyler Sabapathy, 18, "sustained critical injuries" in the fall and passed away Tuesday, Temple University announced.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida Republican introduces bill to add Trump to Mount Rushmore
item.description: The Interior Department would be in charge of undertaking Trump’s addition to the South Dakota landmark if approved by Congress.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump looks to repurpose federal money to expand school choice programs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Private school vouchers and other school choice initiatives would expand under an order coming from President Donald Trump telling government agencies to repurpose federal dollars.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Capitol Police arrest man with Molotov cocktails attempting to target Johnson, Hegseth, Bessent
item.description: The U.S. Capitol Police arrested a man with Molotov cocktails and knives who was allegedly attempting to kill House Speaker Mike Johnson and President Trump’s two recently confirmed nominees, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Honda recalls more than 290,000 vehicles over possible engine issue
item.description: An error in the fuel injection electronic control unit could lead to the engine stalling or a loss os power.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KRON4

item.title: Live updates: White House rescinds funding freeze, RFK Jr. faces tough questions
item.description: President Trump's executive actions, including a freeze on federal grants and a buyout offer to federal employees, have drawn criticism from Democrats and some Republicans, while his pick for HHS Secretary, Robert F. Kennedy, Jr.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, ABC 4

item.title: Trump makes moves to expand power, sparking chaos, possible constitutional crisis
item.description: Just a little over a week into his second term, President Donald Trump is taking steps to maximize his power, sparking chaos and what critics contend is a constitutional crisis as he challenges the separation of powers that have defined American government for more than 200 years.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Exclusive look into Trump repatriation flight on C-17 military plane to Ecuador
item.description: Fox News got exclusive access to a recent repatriation flight on a C-17 military plane, where more than 80 illegal migrants were deported from El Paso, Texas, to Ecuador.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top-tier college student, former high school football star killed in freak accident
item.description: Authorities confirmed that a Clemson University student died after he was crushed and trapped by a fallen tree in a freak accident over the weekend in South Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida man, 88, allegedly killed his wife, attempted to make killing look like a suicide
item.description: An 88-year-old Florida man is facing charges after allegedly killing his wife and staging the scene to make her death appear like a suicide.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sinaloa Cartel's cargo train Nike shoe heist latest in long-standing trend: law enforcement
item.description: The Sinaloa Cartel offloaded $202,500 in Nike merchandise from a BNSF train on Jan. 17, according to the Department of Homeland Security Investigations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: While signing Laken Riley Act, Trump says he'll send 'worst criminal aliens' to Guantanamo
item.description: President Donald Trump on Wednesday used a signing ceremony for the Laken Riley Act to announce that his administration plans to send the “worst criminal aliens” to detention in Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US children fall further behind in reading, make little improvement in math on national exam
item.description: New results from a national exam find that America’s children have continued to lose ground on reading skills in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic and have made little improvement in math.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Brittney Griner plans to sign with the Atlanta Dream, ending her 11-year run with Phoenix
item.description: Brittney Griner plans to sign with the Atlanta Dream, marking the end of her 11-year career with the Phoenix Mercury.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump order aims to end federal support for gender transitions for those under 19
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order aimed at cutting federal support for gender transitions for people under age 19, his latest move to roll back protections for transgender people across the country.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, GEOFF MULVIHILL and HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Head Start and Medicaid providers hit glitches as Trump freezes federal money
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As President Donald Trump paused federal grants and loans in a far-reaching directive, early childhood education centers and states discovered Tuesday they could no longer access money they rely on to provide care for some of the nation's neediest families and children.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fewer than half in new poll trust Trump, RFK Jr. to make right recommendations on health issues
item.description: Fewer than half of Americans in a new poll say they trust President Trump or his health-related nominees to make recommendations on the nation's pressing health care issues.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: A GOP lawsuit to limit people in the US illegally from the census count will be harmful, voters say
item.description: Voters in California and Texas affiliated with a Democratic group want to intervene in a lawsuit filed by four Republican state attorneys general that is attempting to exclude people in the U.S. illegally from numbers used to divvy up congressional seats among states.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why February's Supplemental Security Income checks are coming early
item.description: If you receive Supplemental Security Income from the Social Security Administration, you know the last two payments have been a bit wonky.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: American Airlines worker struck, killed by vehicle at Charlotte Airport
item.description: A worker at Charlotte Douglas International Airport (CLT) died Monday after being struck by an airline ramp vehicle on the tarmac, local police have confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Abby Mittower, KRON4

item.title: Apple software update will activate AI by default, but there is a way to turn it off
item.description: iPhone users could have a somewhat surprising new feature on their devices after their next software update.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: In ‘2000 Meters to Andriivka,’ Oscar winner takes viewers back to Ukraine’s frontlines
item.description: PARK CITY, Utah (AP) — The day Mstyslav Chernov won the BAFTA for his documentary “20 Days in Mariupol” was the day he learned two soldiers he knew had been killed in combat.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: First US case of H5N9 bird flu strain found in California
item.description: The first confirmed case of H5N9 bird flu in the United States has been identified in Merced County, California, and control measures are being undertaken to contain the virus.
item.dc:creator: Dom McAndrew, ABC 4
